details: begin static numerical stability analysis of atan_mean
compute kernel uses ckl::sfpu_atan and ckl::reduce<AVG, REDUCE_ROW> from kernel_lib
AVG/REDUCE_ROW uses matmul path (reduce_uses_matmul=true). Confirmed via reduce_helpers_common.hpp: SUM/AVG along REDUCE_ROW returns true.
reader calls dataflow_kernel_lib::calculate_and_prepare_reduce_scaler<cb_scaler, AVG, REDUCE_ROW, W>(); scaler_f = 1.0/W; bf16 col-0 fill; rounded to bf16 since CB format is Float16_b
ComputeConfigDescriptor: math_fidelity=HiFi4, fp32_dest_acc_en=True; defaults baked in, NOT user-configurable in Phase 0
cb_input_tiles=fp32 (2 pages), cb_scaler=bf16 (1 page), cb_output_tiles=fp32 (2 pages), cb_atan_tiles=fp32 (Wt pages). No UnpackToDestMode override configured for any CB.

atan_tile dispatches calculate_atan with is_fp32_dest_acc_en=true (DST_ACCUM_MODE=true at HiFi4) -> 9-term Sollya minimax polynomial; output left in fp32 in dest (no float_to_fp16b round). No approx_mode parameter on atan; uses APPROX=true via SFPU_INIT_KERNEL_CALL but APPROXIMATION_MODE only controls non-existent code path for atan.

matmul-mode REDUCE_ROW for each row-tile: single tile_regs_acquire/commit/wait around Wt successive reduce_matmul_tiles calls -> Wt*32=W elements accumulated entirely in fp32 dest, no L1 round-trips per row. Output then packed once to fp32 cb_output_tiles.
No max-subtract / ReLU clamp / epsilon needed (atan output bounded |arctan(x)|<pi/2 ~ 1.57, no exp/recip/sqrt/log in flow). No non-tile-aligned masking - entry point enforces W%32==0 and H%32==0.

order-of-operations: scaler is 1/W (divide-then-sum encoded into matmul col-0 scaler). However matmul col-0 path effectively computes sum(x_i)*scaler in fp32 dest per row, so it's mathematically equivalent to sum-then-divide with a single fp32 multiply per accumulated term.

scaler is bf16 (Float16_b CB) — 1/W is rounded to 7-mantissa-bit representation before being used as the matmul column-0 multiplicand each reduction step. This is a fixed precision floor for typical W values.
Phase 0 uses HiFi4 + fp32_dest_acc_en. Reference note for Wormhole B0 mentions HiFi4+fp32_dest may hit bug #38306; flagging as observation but op_design hard-codes HiFi4.
